medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.moa: Central prostaglandin synthesis inhibition with minimal peripheral anti-inflammatory activity.
majorInteractions: Chronic alcohol use increases hepatotoxic risk
Warfarin effect may increase with frequent high doses
monitoring: Total daily dose from all combination products
Liver function when prolonged high-dose use is needed
pearls: Standard adult maximum is often 4 g/day; many institutions use lower limits.

medications.name: Ibuprofen
medications.moa: Reversible cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition reducing prostaglandin-mediated inflammation and pain.
majorInteractions: Anticoagulants increase bleeding risk
ACE inhibitor/diuretic combination may worsen kidney injury
pearls: Use caution in older adults and in volume-depleted patients.

medications.name: Ketorolac
medications.moa: Potent nonselective COX inhibition for short-term analgesia.
indications: Short-term moderate to severe acute pain
majorInteractions: Other NSAIDs increase toxicity
Anticoagulants increase bleeding risk
pearls: Use is generally limited to a short course because toxicity rises with duration.

medications.name: Amoxicillin
medications.moa: Beta-lactam antibiotic that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis.
majorInteractions: Warfarin INR may increase
Allopurinol may increase rash risk
monitoring: Renal function when prolonged therapy is used
pearls: Consider local resistance patterns when selecting empiric therapy.

medications.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate
medications.moa: Amoxicillin inhibits cell wall synthesis; clavulanate inhibits beta-lactamase enzymes.
contraindications: Prior cholestatic jaundice with this combination
majorInteractions: Warfarin INR may increase
Methotrexate clearance may be reduced
pearls: Higher clavulanate content can increase GI adverse effects.

medications.name: Azithromycin
medications.moa: Binds 50S ribosomal subunit to inhibit bacterial protein synthesis.
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging drugs increase arrhythmia risk
Digoxin concentration may increase
monitoring: ECG risk profile in high-risk patients
pearls: Long tissue half-life allows short-course regimens for some indications.

medications.name: Ceftriaxone
medications.moa: Beta-lactam antibiotic that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis.
majorInteractions: Calcium-containing IV solutions in neonates
Warfarin anticoagulant effect may increase
pearls: Broad gram-negative activity makes it common in empiric inpatient regimens.

medications.name: Vancomycin
medications.moa: Inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding D-Ala-D-Ala cell wall precursors.
majorInteractions: Concurrent nephrotoxic agents increase kidney injury risk
Loop diuretics may increase ototoxic risk
pearls: Oral formulation treats gut infection and does not provide systemic coverage.

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.moa: Blocks conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II, reducing vasoconstriction and aldosterone effects.
indications: Renal protection in selected patients with diabetes
majorInteractions: Potassium supplements increase hyperkalemia risk
NSAIDs may blunt effect and worsen renal function
pearls: A creatinine rise can occur after initiation; evaluate trend and clinical context.

medications.name: Amlodipine
medications.moa: Blocks L-type calcium channels in vascular smooth muscle to reduce peripheral vascular resistance.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors may increase concentration
Simvastatin exposure increases with coadministration
monitoring: Heart rate when used with other rate-lowering agents
pearls: Edema is dose-related and may improve with regimen adjustment.

medications.name: Metoprolol Tartrate
medications.moa: Selective beta-1 adrenergic blockade lowering heart rate, contractility, and blood pressure.
majorInteractions: Non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers may cause excessive AV nodal suppression
CYP2D6 inhibitors may increase concentration
pearls: Avoid abrupt discontinuation in patients with ischemic heart disease.

medications.name: Furosemide
medications.moa: Inhibits sodium-potassium-chloride cotransporter in the loop of Henle, promoting natriuresis.
contraindications: Known severe sulfonamide reaction (relative depending on context)
adverseEffects: Ototoxicity with high-dose rapid IV use
majorInteractions: Aminoglycosides increase ototoxic risk
NSAIDs may reduce diuretic response
pearls: IV onset is faster than PO and useful for acute decompensated states.

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.moa: Inhibits hepatic HMG-CoA reductase, reducing cholesterol synthesis and increasing LDL receptor activity.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors increase myopathy risk
Grapefruit products may increase exposure
pearls: Most LDL reduction occurs at moderate doses; intensity can be tailored to risk.

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.moa: Irreversibly inhibits platelet COX-1, reducing thromboxane-mediated platelet aggregation.
contraindications: Children with viral illness due to Reye syndrome risk
majorInteractions: Concurrent anticoagulants increase bleeding risk
NSAIDs can interfere with antiplatelet effect timing
pearls: Use with food or GI protection when bleeding risk is elevated.

medications.name: Heparin
medications.moa: Potentiates antithrombin activity, inhibiting thrombin and factor Xa.
majorInteractions: Antiplatelet agents increase bleeding risk
Other anticoagulants increase bleeding risk
pearls: Short half-life makes UFH useful when rapid reversal flexibility is needed.

medications.name: Enoxaparin
medications.moa: Potentiates antithrombin with predominant anti-factor Xa activity.
majorInteractions: Antiplatelets increase bleeding risk
Other anticoagulants increase bleeding risk
pearls: Renal function strongly influences accumulation risk.

medications.name: Apixaban
medications.moa: Directly inhibits factor Xa to reduce thrombin generation.
majorInteractions: Strong dual CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitors may increase levels
Strong inducers may reduce efficacy
pearls: Routine coagulation monitoring is not required but does not imply no bleeding risk.

medications.name: Insulin Glargine
medications.moa: Basal insulin analog providing prolonged glucose-lowering activity.
indications: Basal insulin replacement in diabetes mellitus
contraindications: Hypoglycemia episode at time of administration
majorInteractions: Other glucose-lowering agents can increase hypoglycemia risk
Beta blockers may mask hypoglycemia symptoms
pearls: Do not mix in the same syringe with rapid-acting insulin.

medications.name: Insulin Lispro
medications.moa: Rapid-onset insulin analog that facilitates peripheral glucose uptake and suppresses hepatic glucose output.
indications: IV insulin protocols in selected inpatient settings
majorInteractions: Other antidiabetic agents increase hypoglycemia risk
Steroids can increase insulin requirements
pearls: Timing with meals is critical to reduce hypoglycemia risk.

medications.name: Metformin
medications.moa: Reduces hepatic gluconeogenesis and improves insulin sensitivity.
adverseEffects: Vitamin B12 deficiency with chronic use
majorInteractions: Iodinated contrast may transiently increase risk of lactic acidosis in select patients
Alcohol excess increases lactic acidosis risk
pearls: Taking with meals improves GI tolerance.

medications.name: Levothyroxine
medications.moa: Synthetic thyroxine (T4) replacement converted peripherally to active triiodothyronine (T3).
majorInteractions: Calcium and iron reduce oral absorption
Warfarin anticoagulant effect may increase
pearls: Separate oral doses from binding agents to preserve absorption.

medications.name: Albuterol
medications.moa: Stimulates beta-2 receptors to relax bronchial smooth muscle.
majorInteractions: Nonselective beta blockers can blunt bronchodilation
Other sympathomimetics increase tachycardia risk
pearls: Frequent rescue use suggests poor baseline control and need for regimen reassessment.

medications.name: Ipratropium-Albuterol
medications.moa: Combines muscarinic antagonism and beta-2 agonism to improve bronchodilation.
majorInteractions: Additional anticholinergics increase anticholinergic effects
Other sympathomimetics increase tachycardia risk
pearls: Often given as scheduled plus PRN in COPD exacerbation pathways.

medications.name: Prednisone
medications.moa: Suppresses inflammatory cytokine signaling and immune-cell activity.
majorInteractions: NSAIDs increase GI irritation risk
CYP3A4 modifiers may alter steroid exposure
pearls: Short courses may still cause glucose elevation and sleep disturbance.

medications.name: Omeprazole
medications.moa: Irreversibly inhibits gastric parietal cell H+/K+ ATPase, reducing acid secretion.
indications: Stress ulcer prophylaxis in selected patients
majorInteractions: May reduce clopidogrel activation
Can reduce absorption of acid-dependent medications
pearls: Use the lowest effective duration to limit long-term adverse effects.

medications.name: Ondansetron
medications.moa: Blocks serotonin 5-HT3 receptors centrally and peripherally to reduce nausea signaling.
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging drugs raise arrhythmia risk
Serotonergic combinations may increase serotonin syndrome risk
monitoring: ECG risk profile in high-risk patients
pearls: Consider ECG risk factors when repeated IV dosing is needed.

medications.name: Senna-Docusate
medications.moa: Senna stimulates colonic motility; docusate lowers stool surface tension to soften stool.
majorInteractions: Other laxatives increase diarrhea risk
Chronic overuse may alter absorption of some oral medications
pearls: A bowel regimen is often paired with scheduled opioid therapy.

medications.name: Sertraline
medications.moa: Selectively inhibits serotonin reuptake in the central nervous system.
majorInteractions: Other serotonergic agents increase serotonin syndrome risk
Anticoagulants/antiplatelets increase bleeding risk
pearls: Therapeutic response is delayed; counsel patients on expected onset.

medications.name: Lorazepam
medications.moa: Enhances GABA-A receptor activity to produce anxiolytic, sedative, and anticonvulsant effects.
contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilatory support
majorInteractions: Opioids significantly increase respiratory depression risk
Other CNS depressants increase sedation
pearls: Use the lowest effective dose and shortest duration feasible.

medications.name: Haloperidol
medications.moa: Primarily dopamine D2 receptor antagonism in central pathways.
indications: Delirium-associated severe agitation in selected settings
contraindications: Parkinson disease with severe motor symptoms (relative)
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging agents increase arrhythmia risk
Dopaminergic medications may have antagonized effects
pearls: Combine with de-escalation and monitor closely for dystonic reactions.

medications.name: Epinephrine
medications.moa: Agonist at alpha and beta adrenergic receptors causing vasoconstriction, bronchodilation, and inotropy/chronotropy.
contraindications: No absolute contraindication in life-threatening anaphylaxis
majorInteractions: Nonselective beta blockers can blunt bronchodilation and worsen alpha-mediated effects
Other sympathomimetics increase arrhythmia risk
pearls: IM thigh administration is preferred first-line for anaphylaxis.

medications.name: Naloxone
medications.moa: Competitive opioid receptor antagonism reversing opioid-induced respiratory depression.
majorInteractions: Opioid agonists have antagonized effects
Partial agonists may require repeated dosing
pearls: Observe for renarcotization after initial response, especially with long-acting opioids.

medications.name: Piperacillin-Tazobactam
medications.moa: Piperacillin inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis while tazobactam inhibits susceptible beta-lactamases.
indications: Complicated intra-abdominal or skin/soft tissue infections
majorInteractions: Concurrent vancomycin may increase nephrotoxicity risk
Methotrexate clearance may be reduced
pearls: Extended-infusion dosing is commonly used in hospitals to optimize pharmacodynamic exposure.

medications.name: Cefepime
medications.moa: Beta-lactam antibiotic that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis.
indications: Severe gram-negative infection including Pseudomonas risk
adverseEffects: Neurotoxicity or encephalopathy in renal dysfunction
majorInteractions: Nephrotoxic agents may increase kidney injury risk
Warfarin anticoagulant effect may increase
pearls: Neurotoxicity risk increases when renal dose adjustment is delayed.

medications.name: Meropenem
medications.moa: Broad-spectrum beta-lactam that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis and is stable to many beta-lactamases.
indications: Severe sepsis with resistant gram-negative concern
adverseEffects: Seizures (rare, risk increased with renal impairment)
majorInteractions: Valproic acid concentrations may decrease significantly
Ganciclovir coadministration may increase seizure risk
pearls: Reserve broad carbapenem coverage for resistant organisms or severe illness when appropriate.

medications.name: Doxycycline
medications.moa: Binds the 30S ribosomal subunit to inhibit bacterial protein synthesis.
indications: Selected skin/soft tissue infections including CA-MRSA
contraindications: Pregnancy or children younger than 8 years (relative depending on indication)
majorInteractions: Calcium, iron, and magnesium products reduce oral absorption
Warfarin anticoagulant effect may increase
pearls: Take with water and remain upright after oral dosing to reduce pill esophagitis risk.

medications.name: Clindamycin
medications.moa: Binds the 50S ribosomal subunit and inhibits bacterial protein synthesis.
indications: Adjunct toxin suppression in severe streptococcal infection
contraindications: History of severe clindamycin-associated C. difficile colitis (relative)
majorInteractions: Neuromuscular blocking agents may have enhanced effects
Concurrent erythromycin may reduce antibacterial activity
pearls: Coverage is strong for many gram-positive and anaerobic pathogens but limited for aerobic gram-negative organisms.

medications.name: Linezolid
medications.moa: Inhibits bacterial protein synthesis by preventing 50S initiation complex formation.
indications: Oral step-down therapy for resistant gram-positive infections
contraindications: Uncontrolled hypertension with potent adrenergic agents (relative)
adverseEffects: Peripheral or optic neuropathy with prolonged use
majorInteractions: Serotonergic medications increase serotonin syndrome risk
Tyramine-rich foods may increase blood pressure
pearls: Excellent oral bioavailability allows IV-to-PO conversion in many stable patients.

medications.name: Nafcillin
medications.moa: Penicillinase-resistant beta-lactam that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis in susceptible organisms.
indications: MSSA endocarditis or deep tissue infection
Serious skin and soft tissue infection due to susceptible staphylococci
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may decrease due to enzyme induction
Methotrexate exposure may increase
pearls: For serious MSSA infections, anti-staphylococcal penicillins are often preferred over vancomycin when feasible.

medications.name: Oxacillin
medications.moa: Penicillinase-resistant beta-lactam that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis in susceptible gram-positive bacteria.
indications: MSSA skin and soft tissue infection
Bone and joint infection caused by susceptible staphylococci
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may decrease
Methotrexate exposure may increase
pearls: A frequent inpatient choice for serious MSSA infection when susceptibilities confirm oxacillin sensitivity.

medications.name: Cloxacillin
medications.moa: Penicillinase-resistant beta-lactam that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis in susceptible organisms.
indications: MSSA skin and soft tissue infection
Step-down oral therapy for susceptible staphylococcal infection in selected settings
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may decrease
Methotrexate exposure may increase
pearls: Oral formulations can be useful for outpatient treatment of susceptible staphylococcal infections.

medications.name: Dicloxacillin
medications.moa: Penicillinase-resistant beta-lactam that inhibits cell wall synthesis in susceptible staphylococci.
indications: MSSA skin and soft tissue infection
Mild to moderate staphylococcal infection suitable for oral therapy
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may decrease
Methotrexate exposure may increase
pearls: Often used as an oral option for confirmed MSSA when MRSA risk is low.

medications.name: Flucloxacillin
medications.moa: Penicillinase-resistant beta-lactam that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis in susceptible staphylococci.
indications: MSSA skin and soft tissue infection
Serious MSSA infection when local protocols support use
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may decrease
Methotrexate exposure may increase
pearls: Use is common in some international guidelines for MSSA where this agent is available.

medications.name: Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole
medications.moa: Blocks sequential bacterial folate synthesis steps via sulfamethoxazole and trimethoprim.
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may increase significantly
ACE inhibitors/ARBs can increase hyperkalemia risk
pearls: Renal dosing and hydration are important to reduce toxicity risk.

medications.name: Clopidogrel
medications.moa: Irreversibly inhibits platelet ADP (P2Y12) receptors to reduce platelet aggregation.
indications: Secondary prevention after ischemic stroke or peripheral arterial disease
majorInteractions: Omeprazole or esomeprazole may reduce activation
Anticoagulants increase bleeding risk
pearls: It is a prodrug requiring CYP2C19 activation, which can vary between patients.

medications.name: Rivaroxaban
medications.moa: Directly inhibits factor Xa to reduce thrombin generation and clot formation.
indications: Postoperative VTE prophylaxis in selected settings
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitors may increase exposure
Strong CYP3A4/P-gp inducers may reduce efficacy
monitoring: Adherence and dose timing with food when required
pearls: Higher-dose regimens are typically taken with food to improve absorption.

medications.name: Warfarin
medications.moa: Inhibits vitamin K epoxide reductase, reducing synthesis of factors II, VII, IX, and X.
indications: Atrial fibrillation stroke prevention in selected patients
contraindications: Inability to perform safe INR monitoring
majorInteractions: Many CYP-modifying drugs can raise or lower INR
Large changes in vitamin K intake alter anticoagulant effect
pearls: Onset is delayed, so bridging strategies may be needed in selected high-risk scenarios.

medications.name: Losartan
medications.moa: Blocks angiotensin II type 1 receptors, reducing vasoconstriction and aldosterone effects.
indications: Heart failure when ACE inhibitors are not tolerated
majorInteractions: Potassium supplements and potassium-sparing diuretics increase hyperkalemia risk
NSAIDs may blunt antihypertensive effect and worsen renal function
pearls: Compared with ACE inhibitors, cough and angioedema are less common but still possible.

medications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
medications.moa: Inhibits sodium-chloride transport in the distal convoluted tubule, increasing natriuresis.
indications: Recurrent calcium nephrolithiasis prevention in selected patients
majorInteractions: Lithium levels may increase
Other antihypertensives may cause additive hypotension
pearls: Diuretic efficacy can fall at lower eGFR, so regimen adjustments may be needed.

medications.name: Spironolactone
medications.moa: Antagonizes mineralocorticoid receptors to reduce sodium retention and potassium loss.
indications: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
Resistant hypertension or ascites in selected patients
majorInteractions: ACE inhibitors/ARBs or potassium supplements increase hyperkalemia risk
Trimethoprim can further increase potassium
pearls: Recheck potassium soon after initiation or dose increases, especially in CKD.

medications.name: Carvedilol
medications.drugClass: Nonselective beta blocker with alpha-1 blockade
medications.moa: Blocks beta-1, beta-2, and alpha-1 receptors to reduce heart rate, contractility, and afterload.
indications: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
contraindications: Severe bradycardia or high-grade AV block without pacing
Cardiogenic shock or decompensated severe heart failure
majorInteractions: Non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers may cause excessive AV nodal suppression
Insulin or hypoglycemic therapies may have masked adrenergic warning symptoms
monitoring: Heart failure symptoms and volume status
pearls: Taking with food can reduce orthostatic symptoms during titration.

medications.name: Diltiazem
medications.moa: Blocks L-type calcium channels in myocardium, AV node, and vascular smooth muscle.
contraindications: Decompensated heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
majorInteractions: Beta blockers can increase risk of bradycardia or heart block
CYP3A4 interactions can alter exposure of diltiazem or coadministered drugs
pearls: Use caution in reduced-EF heart failure because of negative inotropic effects.

medications.name: Nitroglycerin
medications.moa: Releases nitric oxide to increase cGMP, producing venodilation and reduced myocardial oxygen demand.
majorInteractions: PDE-5 inhibitors can cause profound hypotension
Alcohol and other antihypertensives may enhance hypotension
pearls: Nitrate-free intervals are needed for chronic nitrate formulations to limit tolerance.

medications.name: Pantoprazole
medications.moa: Irreversibly inhibits gastric parietal cell H+/K+ ATPase to suppress acid production.
indications: Upper GI bleed regimens in selected protocols
Stress ulcer prophylaxis in selected patients
majorInteractions: Can reduce absorption of acid-dependent medications
High-dose methotrexate exposure may increase
monitoring: Magnesium and B12 in long-term therapy
pearls: Often preferred when minimizing CYP2C19-related interaction concerns.

medications.name: Lactulose
medications.moa: Nonabsorbable disaccharide that draws water into the colon and acidifies stool to trap ammonia.
majorInteractions: Other laxatives can complicate hepatic encephalopathy titration
Broad-spectrum antibiotics may reduce ammonia-lowering effect
pearls: For hepatic encephalopathy, dosing is often titrated to 2-3 soft stools per day.

medications.name: Insulin Regular
medications.moa: Binds insulin receptors to increase cellular glucose uptake and suppress hepatic glucose production.
indications: Prandial and correction insulin in selected regimens
Diabetic ketoacidosis or hyperosmolar crisis IV protocols
majorInteractions: Other glucose-lowering agents increase hypoglycemia risk
Corticosteroids may increase insulin requirements
pearls: It is the insulin most commonly used for IV infusion protocols such as DKA management.

medications.name: Glucagon
medications.moa: Activates hepatic glycogenolysis and gluconeogenesis to raise blood glucose.
indications: Severe hypoglycemia when oral intake is not possible
Adjunct in beta blocker or calcium channel blocker toxicity
GI radiology smooth muscle relaxation adjunct
majorInteractions: Warfarin anticoagulant effect may increase
Beta blockers may produce a more pronounced blood pressure or heart rate response
monitoring: Recurrence of hypoglycemia after initial reversal
pearls: Give oral carbohydrate when alert to replenish glycogen stores after rescue dosing.
